properties.name: High Impervious Surface — Honolulu Urban Core (60-100%)
properties.flood_risk_increase: 40-60% higher peak flows vs permeable baseline
properties.desc: Downtown Honolulu with extensive pavement, rooftops, and impervious surfaces. 75%+ coverage means virtually all rainfall becomes surface runoff. March 23 rain bomb overwhelmed stormwater infrastructure.
properties.climate_impact: Urban heat island effect increases local convection; contributes to stationary rainfall cells over developed areas.

properties.name: Medium Impervious Surface — Waialua Town (30-50%)
properties.stormwater_capacity: Rural drainage inadequate for extreme events
properties.desc: Waialua-Haleiwa area. Mix of agricultural land with increasing residential development. Inadequate stormwater infrastructure for the scale of 2026 event (8-12 inches in 36 hours).
properties.satellite_note: ESA Urbinsule project shows expanding impervious surface in this region since 2010.

properties.name: Low Impervious Surface — West Maui Agricultural (10-25%)
properties.stormwater_capacity: Natural infiltration + drainage; overwhelmed by 62 inch total
properties.desc: Historic pineapple and agriculture zone. Lower impervious coverage but HIGH erosion risk due to iron-rich pineapple soils and post-wildfire conditions. 62 inches rain mobilized debris flows despite low urban coverage.
properties.pineapple_soil_note: Molokai/Mahiki series soils in this zone have 24-30% Fe2O3; iron oxide creates low permeability despite agricultural use.
